title: Transitional Mental Health Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Transitional Mental Health Services 3 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LIFEWAYS INC COMMUNITY SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a continuum of care for people with mental health challenges, designed to help consumers move toward maximum recovery and successful community life. Services include individual and group therapy, skill development, targeted case management, hospital discharge services, dual diagnosis treatment, peer-delivered services, representative payee services, residential and 20-hour personal care services, and coordinated care services. Most services are provided at client's residence. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Diagnosis of severe mental illness and deficits in at least two life domains Hours: Monday-Wednesday 8am-6pm, Thursday and Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 889-9167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRILLIUM FAMILY SERVICES YOUNG ADULT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A voluntary residential program for individuals struggling with significant mental health diagnoses as they transition toward independent living. Program encourages independent living and helps clients manage the balance of everyday life in conjunction with self-care. 3415 SE Powell Boulevard Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Ages 17-24 who need temporary guidance as they develop the skills necessary to manage their mental health needs with daily life expectations Hours: Follows school district hours with an extended calendar Email (503) 813-7760 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWAYS INC COMMUNITY SUPPORT SERVICES OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER TRILLIUM FAMILY SERVICES YOUNG ADULT PROGRAM
